title: Maximilian d'Asburgo column, Venice
tags: castello giardinidellabiennale italia italy venezia venice
description:
Maximilian d'Asburgo column in Giardini Napoleonici, Venice, Italy, 17 May 2025

A rostral column is a type of victory column originating in ancient Greece and Rome, where they were erected to commemorate a naval military victory. Its defining characteristic is the integrated prows or rams of ships, representing captured or destroyed enemy ships.

This rostral column was commissioned by the Austria-Hungarian navy in honour of Vice Admiral Archduke Maximillian and completed in 1876. It commemorates the Battle of Lissa in 1866 where the Austrian Navy defeated the Italian Navy.  It stood in Pula (now in Croatia) which was Austria’s primary naval base on the Adriatic from 1853 to 1918. 

Following the defeat of Austria-Hungary in November 1918, Italy occupied the Istrian peninsula, where Pula sits. This column was taken as booty and shipped to Venice to be re-erected here.

tags: veniceoffthebeatenpath venisehorsdessentiersbattus castellodistrictinvenice giardinipubblici venicegardens jardinsàvenise giuseppegaribaldi giuseppegaribaldimonument statueofgiuseppegaribaldi monumentdegiuseppegaribaldi statuedegiuseppegaribaldi michelgauthier nikond7100 dxophotolab venezia vénétie lasérénissime républiquedevenise républiquesérénissime
description:
Travel Memories / Souvenirs de voyage

(French follows)

This peaceful garden is part of the Public Gardens (Giardini Pubblici in Italian) of Venice located in Castello, on the extreme southeast side of the district. These gardens were laid out by order of Napoleon, and somewhere in the 19th century they were landscaped in the spirit of the elegance typical of the English style gardens. A particular eye-catching landmark of the Public Gardens refers to the Giuseppe Garibaldi Monument (1885), located at one end of the park.

************* 

Ce paisible jardin fait partie des Jardins Publics de Venise, situés à Castello, à l'extrême sud-est du quartier. Aménagés sur ordre de Napoléon, ils furent aménagés au XIXe siècle dans l'esprit de l'élégance typique des jardins à l'anglaise. Le monument à Giuseppe Garibaldi (1885), situé à l'une des extrémités du parc, est un monument particulièrement remarquable.

description:
Italy, Venice, Giardini della Biennale, the Restaurant/Café InParadiso at a lovely quiet green side, a place to relax in the tranquillity after visiting overcrowded places. The panoramic terrace with cosy sofas overlooking the entire basin of San Marco, especially offers a wonderful view of the lagoon for a magical sunset justifying the name InParadise.

The cuisine of InParadise ranges from typical Venetian dishes to more creative tasty finger food, aperitifs, refreshments or just an espresso or cappuccino.

The lagoon city hides many picturesque places offside the tourist roads. If the buildings would be straight, painted & well maintained, I think Venice without this patina, the gondolas & gondolieri, channels, little bridges & the at all-time busy Canal Grande; all these main ingredients altogether express the romantic charm of Venice.  

👉 One World one Dream,
🙏...Danke, Xièxie 谢谢, Thanks, Gracias, Merci, Grazie, Obrigado, Arigatô, Dhanyavad, Chokrane to you & over 
16  million visits in my photostream with countless motivating comments

description: Biennalist @ Venice Biennale during the Venice Biennale 2019 Biennalist format will express the Biennale concept with art work Biennalist / Venice Biennale www.emergencyrooms.org/biennalist.html #ThierryGeoffroy #venicebiennale #biennalist #artformat #biennale #artbiennale #biennial #BiennaleArte2019 more here about the Biennale : Ralph Rugoff has declared: «May You Live in Interesting Times will no doubt include artworks that reflect upon precarious aspects of existence today, including different threats to key traditions, institutions and relationships of the “post-war order.” But let us acknowledge at the outset that art does not exercise its forces in the domain of politics. Art cannot stem the rise of nationalist movements and authoritarian governments in different parts of the world, for instance, nor can it alleviate the tragic fate of displaced peoples across the globe (whose numbers now represent almost one percent of the world’s entire population).» ALBANIA Maybe the cosmos is not so extraordinary Commissioner: Ministry of Culture Republic of Albania. description: Biennalist @ Venice Biennale during the Venice Biennale 2019 Biennalist format will express the Biennale concept with art work Biennalist / Venice Biennale www.emergencyrooms.org/biennalist.html #ThierryGeoffroy #venicebiennale #biennalist #artformat #biennale #artbiennale #biennial #BiennaleArte2019 more here about the Biennale : Ralph Rugoff has declared: «May You Live in Interesting Times will no doubt include artworks that reflect upon precarious aspects of existence today, including different threats to key traditions, institutions and relationships of the “post-war order.” But let us acknowledge at the outset that art does not exercise its forces in the domain of politics. Art cannot stem the rise of nationalist movements and authoritarian governments in different parts of the world, for instance, nor can it alleviate the tragic fate of displaced peoples across the globe (whose numbers now represent almost one percent of the world’s entire population).» ALBANIA Maybe the cosmos is not so extraordinary Commissioner: Ministry of Culture Republic of Albania. description: Biennalist @ Venice Biennale during the Venice Biennale 2019 Biennalist format will express the Biennale concept with art work Biennalist / Venice Biennale www.emergencyrooms.org/biennalist.html #ThierryGeoffroy #venicebiennale #biennalist #artformat #biennale #artbiennale #biennial #BiennaleArte2019 more here about the Biennale : Ralph Rugoff has declared: «May You Live in Interesting Times will no doubt include artworks that reflect upon precarious aspects of existence today, including different threats to key traditions, institutions and relationships of the “post-war order.” But let us acknowledge at the outset that art does not exercise its forces in the domain of politics. Art cannot stem the rise of nationalist movements and authoritarian governments in different parts of the world, for instance, nor can it alleviate the tragic fate of displaced peoples across the globe (whose numbers now represent almost one percent of the world’s entire population).» ALBANIA Maybe the cosmos is not so extraordinary Commissioner: Ministry of Culture Republic of Albania. title: Nuotatrice
tags: carolefeuerman venezia nuotatrice costume cuffia statua arte
description:
Carole A. Feuerman è nota come una delle più rinomate, influenti e popolari scultrici iperrealista al mondo. La sua ricca carriera si estende su quattro decenni durante i quali ha fatto capo a nuovi approcci alla scultura. Lavorando sia a grandezza naturale sia in scala monumentale, è l’unica artista figurativa a dipingere su bronzo, in maniera iperrealistica ed è l’unica ad aver installato queste opere nell’acqua.
Tutto è perfetto nel mondo della Feuerman, ma nulla è scontato. I suoi personaggi non sono pura decorazione ma esprimono un’interiorità che va oltre la iniziale maschera di perfetta serenità.

title: Venice: an Arctic tragedy
tags: park city venice italy sculpture statue europa europe italia eu venise venezia venedig arcticexpedition tamronaf18270mmf3563 nikond5300 ©peterdenton francescooverini giordinedellabiennale
description:
It was the facial expression – not to mention the unusual legwear – that attracted me to this statue, which stands in Venice’s Giordine della Biennale (Biennale Gardens). A little research reveals that it represents Francesco Overini, an explorer who set off on an Arctic expedition in 1900, and never returned. The legwear therefore makes perfect sense – in those days the bandaging was used as (meagre) protection against frostbite.

In 1905 the statue in Overini’s memory was erected in “pride and sorrow” by Prince Luigi Amedeo, Duke of Abruzzi, a mountaineer and explorer who led the expedition.

title: Relationship LXX left in Venice
tags: venice italy streetart sexy collage trash recycling left trashy mycollages rayjohnsonfanclub
description: Ray Johnson Fan Club Relationship LXX ~ "I DON'T NEED TO love EVERYTHING" This 70th collage was completed on 8/19/2013 using trash found on the streets of Venice. I made it overnight at our rental flat on Fondamenta San Giuseppe. It was made on the album cover "Parade des Succes No. 31." This collage was left the same morning near the entrance to Fondamenta San Giuseppe in Venice, Italy. See the original artwork in the photo below. Do you LIKE me? www.facebook.com/rayjohnsonfanclub USING TRASH OFF THE STREETS TO MAKE ART ON THE STREETS. I use only trash and found items to make these one-of-a-kind, hand cut and paste, signed & numbered artworks. I use trashy paperback book covers, record & magazine covers, CDs, posters, postcards and other mass-produced media as a base for my hand-cut & paste collages, which I usually leave out on the streets for anyone to enjoy (or destroy.) Find out more at: www.rayjohnsonfanclub.com SERIOUSLY TRASHY STREET ART!

title: Venetian Lizard
tags: venice italy europe reptile lizard podarcissicula italianwalllizard d300s 1685mmf3556gvr 1685mmvr
description:
Being on another continent doesn't stop me from photographing wildlife.  These little lizards were all over the place in the exceptionally rare Venetian park areas.  They were very fast however and hard to get close to.  This little lizard is clearly a step slower than his brethren as not only was I able to sneak up near to him but if you look closely you'll notice he's lost part of his tail (an evolutionary adaption to help stop him from being eaten) to a predator of some kind indicating a close call in the recent past. 
I believe this is an Italian Wall lizard, Podarcis sicula, but if any European herpetologists can definitively ID it for me it would be appreciated
comments:
lachesis2005: That one looks like a Podarcis muralis rather than a sicula.
Nice shot and timing.

title: Venice , people would say
tags: park bridge venice trees italy geotagged boats evening canal venezia castello hdr giardini pubblici janusz leszczynski 002112 peoplewouldsay geo:lat=45430255 geo:lon=12355229
description: Venice , people would say , that is full of tourists , canals stink and it's very expensive … they could be right but for me it's still possibly most interesting city I have seen. I stayed in cheap ( as for prices there ) hotel but in great location – after all I was not there to stay in the hotel. Food , I like good food but I'm not picky – it can be expensive there , but guess they have supermarkets too. Canals … they didn't stink to me but it's old city … it has the aroma . Tourists .. I was one of them :-) … same like all the other popular places , go early , stay late … if you want to document all “must see” buy an album … Amazon has plenty. If you really want to take great pictures … return after you digested the first trip … this is my plan. … ahhh one more thing … take your time Please View Large See where this picture was taken, Venice ( Castello ) , Italy [?]
